title: Tracker-neutral ticket plan (legacy)
description: LEGACY: a tracker-agnostic standalone test plan, retired as a produced artifact when test-spec folded into spec-kit (its job is now split between build-plan's inline tddCases and the acceptance-plan). Retained so a pre-existing ticket-plan.yaml can still be published. No spec-kit agent produces one. The plan holds INTENT only; routing lives in per-team publisher config.

version.description: Schema version of this plan. Bump only on breaking schema changes.

spec.description: Filename of the companion human-readable test specification (e.g. TEST_SPEC.md).

title.description: Human title for the plan, e.g. 'Test suite for feedback scheduling'.

milestone.description: Single top-level grouping for all tickets. Adapters map this to a Linear Project/Milestone, a Jira Epic, or a GitHub Milestone.

key.description: Stable, unique, kebab-case identifier for the milestone. Used for idempotent re-publish.

tickets.description: The flat list of tickets. Hierarchy and ordering are expressed via the optional `parent` and `blockedBy` relationships, which adapters translate into each tracker's native structure (sub-issues, sub-tasks, links, or checklists).

ticketKey.description: Stable, unique, kebab-case identifier for a ticket. MUST be unique across the plan. This is the load-bearing identity: it is referenced by `parent`/`blockedBy`, and it is STAMPED onto the created tracker issue (as a label or a description marker) so re-publishing updates the existing issue instead of duplicating it.

title.description: Concise human title. Prefix with the layer for scannability, e.g. 'Unit: recurrence/date math'.

layer.description: Where this ticket sits in the test pyramid, or its supporting role.

stack.description: Stack/area key for polyglot repos (e.g. 'python', 'web', 'go-api'). Omit or use '-' for single-stack repos. Adapters may map this to a component/label.

priority.description: Neutral priority. Adapters map to the tracker's scale via per-team config.

estimate.description: Neutral effort estimate (story points). Optional; adapters drop it where unsupported.

labels.description: Free-form neutral labels. Adapters may prefix/translate them.

description.description: Markdown body: why this ticket exists and what it covers. Adapters render this plus `cases`/`modulesInScope`/`acceptanceCriteria` into the issue body.

cases.description: Enumerated test cases as 'input -> expected', including edges and the failure modes from testing-standards.md. Required for test layers (unit/integration/component/e2e); optional for refactor/harness/ci.

modulesInScope.description: REAL, verified file paths an implementer will touch or read: the system-under-test module(s), the test file to create (with the correct naming infix for its runner/environment), and any shared helpers/fixtures/config. Never placeholders.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Checkable Definition of Done as a checklist — not a restatement of the cases. Adapters render this as a task list in the issue body.

parent.description: Optional key of a parent ticket. A RELATIONSHIP, not a structure: the Linear adapter renders it as a sub-issue, Jira as a sub-task, GitHub as a checklist item under the parent. Must reference another ticket's key in this plan.

blockedBy.description: Keys of tickets that must be done first (e.g. a prerequisite refactor or harness setup). Adapters map to 'blocked by' relations/links, or text where unsupported. Each entry must reference another ticket's key in this plan.

allOf.description: Test-layer tickets must enumerate at least one case.
